summary: WinForms-specific ReactiveUI guidance focused on synchronization context usage and reactive form/view patterns.
recommendedPatterns: Use ReactiveForm<TViewModel> or reactive control base types.
Keep bindings in WhenActivated so they follow control lifetime.
Use the WinForms platform scheduler for UI updates.
avoidPatterns: Do not embed business logic in event handlers when a ReactiveCommand belongs in the ViewModel.
Do not assume bindings clean themselves up without disposal.
setupSteps: Create reactive forms and controls for view integration.
commonPitfalls: Updating controls from background work without marshaling to the UI thread.

harvest.sourceSummary: Curated from local ReactiveUI repositories, official GitHub documentation, and current SDK/package guidance.
harvest.notes: Prepared for the ReactiveUI MCP Server knowledge catalog.
